mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by increasing non-farm payrolls, signals a healthy and expanding economy. This improvement in labor market conditions boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows a consistent positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at historically low levels (near zero), it signals an accommodative monetary policy stance. This environment encourages risk-taking and drives equity valuations higher due to lower discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for a sustained period (e.g., 12 months), but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) during this time.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales growth and declining unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and improving labor market conditions. These factors together signal a strong economic expansion, which is highly supportive of equity prices and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If both conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, a significant month-over-month drop in the unemployment rate signals a turning point in the labor market. This improvement is a strong positive for economic recovery and equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops by more than 0.3 percentage points in a month while the rate is above 8%, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing activity and business investment. This sustained expansion in the supply side of the economy translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained positive month-over-month growth for 9 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates renewed confidence in the housing market and broader economic activity. This typically precedes or accompanies periods of equity market strength, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently exceeds 600 thousand units for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and stable, and GDP growth (GDPC1) is accelerating, it creates an ideal 'goldilocks' scenario for equities. Corporate earnings are strong without immediate pressure from rising costs or interest rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month % change < 2.5% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter % change (annualized) > 3%
failure_condition: If both conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 35% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction or recession, a significant rebound in GDP growth signals the end of the downturn and the beginning of a recovery. This is typically a strong bullish signal for equities, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (GDPC1 previous quarter was negative)
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive growth after a negative quarter, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year period.
